author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tomāra gambhīra mana, nāhi bujhe anya jana,
sei mana⋅anusāri’ saba
jagat⋅udbhava⋅sthiti, pralaya⋅saṁsāragati,
mukti⋅ādi śaktira vaibhava

e saba vaidika līlā, icchāmātra prakāśilā,
jīvera vāsanā⋅anusāre
tomāte vimukha ha’ye, majila avidyā la’ye,
sei jīva karma⋅pārābāre

punaḥ jadi bhakti kari’, bhaje bhaktasaṅga dhari’,
tabe pāya tomāra caraṇa
antaraṅga⋅līlārase, bhāse, māyā nā paraśe,
bhaktivinodera phire mana
en_translation:
O Lord Hari! No other person comprehends Your profound will. Following that will, all the creation, maintenance, and destruction of the universe, the course of transmigration, and the opulence of powers like liberation originate.

All these Vedic pastimes You manifest by Your mere will, in accordance with the desires of the individual soul. Becoming averse to You, that soul becomes absorbed in ignorance in the ocean of karma.

Again, if one performs devotion and worships by taking the association of devotees, then they attain Your lotus feet. They float in the mellow of Your intimate pastimes, and illusion does not touch them. Bhaktivinoda's mind returns.
